channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇹 Rubio Questions Allies Support on Iran After Italy Talks
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio met Italian PM Giorgia Meloni and questioned why allies including Italy were not backing Washington efforts to confront Iran and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, highlighting strains in the US-Europe relationship over the Iran conflict.

item.title: 🇹🇼🇨🇳 Taiwan Parliament Approves Extra Defense Spending
item.description: Taiwans opposition-controlled parliament approved extra defense spending of $25 billion, about two-thirds of what the government sought, to bolster armed forces facing a rapidly modernizing Chinese military.

item.title: 🇺🇳⚖️ US Revises UN Resolution on Iran But China Russia Still Expected to Veto
item.description: Washington has revised its proposed UN resolution demanding Iran halt attacks and mining in the Strait of Hormuz, but the changes are unlikely to avert Chinese and Russian vetoes, diplomats said.

item.title: 🇬🇧🇨🇳 British MPs to Visit China This Month for First Time in Seven Years
item.description: A cross-party delegation of British lawmakers will visit China this month, in a sign of warming ties after Beijing agreed to lift sanctions on six MPs following Prime Minister Keir Starmers January visit.

item.title: 📉🇪🇺 European Shares Slide as Middle East Tensions Flare
item.description: European shares slipped on Friday in broad-based losses as a flare-up in the Middle East conflict hit risk sentiment at the end of a week dominated by geopolitics.

item.title: 🇺🇦🇷🇺 Russia and Ukraine Trade Ceasefire Accuses Despite Three-Day Truce Deal
item.description: Russia and Ukraine accused each other of violating ceasefires as Moscow prepared for its Victory Day parade, while Trump announced both sides agreed to a 3-day ceasefire and prisoner swap.

item.title: 🇺🇦⚠️ Ukraine Running Short of Air Defence Missiles After Winter Campaign
item.description: Ukraine's air force warned it is running short of air defence missiles after Russia's massive winter attack campaign, as the country braces for further strikes.

item.title: 🇬🇧🗳️ UK Elections: Reform UK Surges as Labour Suffers Major Losses
item.description: Nigel Farage's hard-right Reform UK party made significant gains in England's local elections while Keir Starmer's Labour Party suffered major losses, with pro-independence parties now governing three of the UK's four nations.

item.title: 🇬🇧 Starmer Vows to Stay On Despite Labour Election Disaster
item.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer insisted he would not resign after disastrous local elections saw Labour lose thousands of council seats and control of major cities including Birmingham, while Reform UK made historic gains.

item.title: ⛪ Pope Leo XIV Marks First Year Amid Tensions With Trump
item.description: Pope Leo XIV sought to focus on a pastoral role in his first year as pontiff, but found himself in verbal sparring matches with Trump over global tensions and Vatican policy.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇱🇧 US to Host New Israel-Lebanon Talks on Hezbollah Disarmament
item.description: The Trump administration presses for Hezbollah disarmament as it awaits Iran response to a new US proposal aimed at advancing peace in the region.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇬🇧 Beijing Condemns UK Conviction of Two Men Tied to Hong Kong Trade Office
item.description: China's foreign ministry accused the UK of abusing the law after Bill Yuen and Peter Wai were found guilty of spying on Hong Kong activists in Britain.

item.title: 🇮🇶 Iraq's Sadr Calls to Exclude Armed Groups From Next Government
item.description: Shia cleric Muqtada al-Sadr urges exclusion of armed groups from Iraq's next government as Zaidi attempts to form a cabinet amid US pressure to curb Iran-backed actors.

item.title: 🇳🇴 Norway holds Chinese woman in custody on suspected espionage charges
item.description: A Norwegian court ruled that a Chinese woman suspected of complicity in espionage will be held in police custody for up to four weeks while authorities investigate the case.

item.title: 💻 US suspects Nvidia chips smuggled to China via Thailand in billions-dollar scheme
item.description: A firm linked to Thailand national AI initiative is suspected of helping smuggle Super Micro servers with advanced Nvidia chips to China, Bloomberg reported, amid ongoing US-China tech tensions.

item.title: 🇬🇷 Greek authorities investigate Ukrainian-made naval drone found in island cave
item.description: Police and coast guard sources are investigating a drone boat discovered by fishermen in a cave on the Ionian island of Lefkada, raising questions about its origin and purpose.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China exports accelerate in April as Iran war drives global stockpiling
item.description: Chinese export growth likely quickened in April as companies worldwide rushed to secure components amid fears the Iran conflict could push input costs higher, a Reuters poll shows.

item.title: 🇩🇪 German industrial production unexpectedly falls 0.7% in March
item.description: German industrial output dropped unexpectedly in March, decreasing 0.7% from the previous month, the federal statistics office reported, signaling continued weakness in Europe largest economy.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 US Expects Iranian Response to Peace Proposal as Fighting Flares in Gulf
item.description: The United States expects an Iranian response as soon as Friday to its latest proposal to end the war, even as US and Iranian forces traded fire and the UAE came under renewed attack.

item.title: 💰🇺🇸 Dollar Slips as Investors Pin Hopes on US-Iran Peace Resolution
item.description: The dollar slipped Friday, on pace for a second straight weekly fall as investors stayed cautiously optimistic about a swift end to the Middle East conflict after Trump said the ceasefire remained in place.

item.title: 🇬🇧🗳️ Questions Mount Over UK PM Starmer Future After Election Losses
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer has vowed to carry on as leader after his Labour Party suffered heavy losses to the populist Reform party, deepening doubts over his ability to govern.

item.title: 🇷🇺🇪🇺 Kremlin Says Putin Ready to Negotiate With EU But Wont Make First Move
item.description: President Vladimir Putin is ready to negotiate with everyone including the Europeans, the Kremlin said, after reports EU leaders were preparing for potential talks.

item.title: 🌍📉 Global Perceptions of US Fall Below Russia Under Trump, Survey Finds
item.description: Global perceptions of the US have deteriorated for a second consecutive year and are now worse than views of Russia, an annual study showed, as Trumps policies continue to strain the NATO alliance.

item.title: 🇹🇷 Turkey-Iran Foreign Ministers Discuss Status of US-Iran Talks
item.description: Turkish Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan held a phone call with Iranian counterpart Abbas Araghchi to review the status of negotiations between Washington and Tehran amid fragile ceasefire.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇱 US Opposes Israeli West Bank Annexation, Diplomat Tells UN
item.description: US ambassador to UN confirms Washington opposes annexation plans, saying regular dialogue continues with partners about West Bank stability and security.
